subject: Chemistry
title: Mithridatism: Immunizing Against Poison
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Mithridatism: Immunizing Against Poison.

hook.text: King Mithridates VI took small doses of poison daily to build immunity. When he tried to kill himself, the poison failed.

buildup.text: He ruled Pontus from 120–63 BC and feared assassination by poison more than anything.

discovery.text: Mithridatism works for some toxins. The body produces enzymes and antibodies that neutralize small, repeated doses.

twist.text: When Rome defeated him, Mithridates tried to poison himself. His tolerance was so high that the poison didn't work.

climax.text: He had to ask a guard to stab him instead. The man who feared poison died by the sword.

punchline.text: Small doses build tolerance. He survived his own suicide attempt.

quiz.question: Why couldn't King Mithridates poison himself when he wanted to die?
options: Years of taking small doses had built up his tolerance to poison
He accidentally took the wrong poison
His servants switched the poison for a harmless liquid
